channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Tech AI Investments in Gulf States Face Risks Amid Regional Conflicts
item.description: The United States’ major technology firms, including Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, have invested heavily in building AI data centres across the Gulf region, aiming to establish it as a global hub for...

item.title: False therapist jailed for 11 years for sexually assaulting and deceiving patient under guise of treatment
item.description: A 77-year-old man named Gerald Peck has been sentenced to 11 years in prison at Lewes Crown Court after being convicted of three counts of sexual assault and two counts of assault by penetration. Peck...

item.title: Tunisian Director Kaouther Ben Hania’s Film ‘The Voice of Hind Rajab’ Portrays Gaza Crisis Through Red Crescent Volunteers
item.description: The Voice of Hind Rajab, a film by Tunisian director Kaouther Ben Hania, has captured global attention after premiering at the Venice Film Festival in 2025 with a 23-minute standing ovation and seven ...

item.title: Housing tax breaks heavily favour wealthy electorates, costing billions
item.description: Property tax concessions in Australia, including the 50 per cent capital gains tax (CGT) discount and negative gearing, are projected to cost the federal budget over $200 billion in the next decade. R...

item.title: Preliminary report suggests outdated US intelligence likely caused deadly strike on Iranian school
item.description: A preliminary investigation has indicated that the United States military was 'likely responsible' for a missile strike on Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab, southern Iran, which killed mor...

item.title: ANC distances itself from Bernice Swartz’s bread donation campaign
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) has publicly reprimanded Deputy Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Bernice Swartz, following her recent bread donation campaign, which has drawn c...

item.title: Africa's Opportunity to Grow Manufacturing and the Structural Challenges It Faces
item.description: Despite Africa's strong population growth, favourable economic projections, and a global reshoring trend, the continent remains largely absent from the global manufacturing boom. Africa represents 18....

item.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to temporary ceasefire at Torkham to retrieve body of Afghan national amid ongoing border clashes
item.description: On Thursday, border forces of Pakistan and Afghanistan agreed to a short break in their ongoing clashes at the Torkham border crossing in order to identify and retrieve a dead body that had been lying...

item.title: Carlton Blues edge Richmond in nail-biting AFL clash
item.description: Carlton managed to recover their momentum with a narrow win over Richmond, 10.15 (75) to 9.17 (71), in a thrilling AFL clash at the MCG with over 74,000 fans in attendance. Despite not scoring a goal ...

item.title: PM Modi assures measures to manage LPG supply amid global energy crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed concerns about the ongoing energy situation in India, which has been affected by the escalating conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. Speaking ...

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Issues First Statement Amid Rising Tensions in Middle East
item.description: On March 12, 2026, Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei delivered his first public statement following the death of his father. Speaking on state television, he strongly condemned the United Sta...

item.title: Government Assures Steady LPG Supply Amid Global Fuel Disruptions
item.description: New Delhi: Amid growing public concern over potential LPG shortages, the Indian government has urged citizens to refrain from panic bookings and conserve fuel. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, cau...

item.title: China Introduces Zero-Tariff Trade Policy for 53 African Countries: Benefits and Risks
item.description: In February 2026, China’s President Xi Jinping announced that from 1 May 2026, China would give zero-tariff access to exports from 53 African countries, leaving out only Eswatini. China-Africa trade h...

item.title: Iran conflict triggers historic disruption in global oil supply
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has caused an unprecedented disruption in global oil markets, affecting 7.5% of worldwide supply and a larger portion of exports, according to the International Energy Age...

item.title: Some Countries, Including India, Hesitate to Accept Tinubu's Ambassador Nominees Due to Short Remaining Term
item.description: India and several other countries dey hesitate or outright reject some of the ambassadors wey President Bola Tinubu nominate recently. The main reason be say Tinubu administration get less than two ye...

item.title: Rwandan Teams Gear Up for 2026 WRC Safari Rally in Naivasha
item.description: Rwandan rally drivers are heading into the 2026 WRC Safari Rally Kenya with strong optimism. The event, set for March 12-15 in Naivasha, Nakuru County, is the third round of the FIA World Rally Champi...

item.title: India Grants Docking Permission to Iranian Ships, IRIS Lavan Arrives in Kochi
item.description: India has officially approved Iran’s request for docking three of its ships, with IRIS Lavan being the first to dock at Kochi port, as confirmed by a spokesperson from the Ministry of External Affairs...

item.title: Sunrisers Leeds acquire Abrar Ahmed despite Pakistan-India cricket tensions
item.description: Pakistani leg-spinner Abrar Ahmed has been signed by Sunrisers Leeds for the 2026 edition of The Hundred, a 100-ball cricket competition in England and Wales, despite concerns about political tensions...

item.title: Understanding Skin Mites: Natural Residents of Healthy Skin
item.description: Almost all humans carry microscopic mites on their skin, which live within hair follicles and sebaceous glands, feeding on skin oils and dead cells. These mites, primarily Demodex folliculorum and Dem...

item.title: 16-Year-Old Liana France Becomes Australia's Youngest Female Winter Paralympian
item.description: Sixteen-year-old Liana France has made history as Australia’s youngest female Winter Paralympian during the Milano Cortina 2026 Games. Making her debut in the women’s giant slalom standing event, Fran...
